commentaries.author: Bede
commentaries.content: And there was given unto him a mouth speaking great things and blasphemies; and power was given unto him to continue forty and two months. blasphemies towards: For he does not blaspheme with open mouth, until three years and a half, but in the mystery of iniquity, which, when a difference is made, and the man of sin is revealed, will be disclosed. And then he will say, "I am Christ;" whereas now it is, "Lo, here is Christ," or, "Lo there. " Again, "towards God" signifies "against God. " <a.

commentaries.author: Bede
commentaries.content: mouth: "Who is exalted," he says, "above all that is called God."

commentaries.author: Pseudo-Dionysius the Areopagite
commentaries.content: "And there was given unto him "he says, "a mouth speaking great things, and blasphemy; and power was given unto him, and forty and two months."

methodology.step_1: Parse main page with BeautifulSoup for structure
methodology.step_3: Use Firecrawl to get full content from 'Go to Commentary' links
methodology.step_4: Fallback to BeautifulSoup for accordion content if needed
advantages: Records verses even without commentaries for complete tracking
